summary: Iteration 4 testing complete. Tested all 7 reported issues. Backend: 19/19 new tests pass. Frontend: Rename flows (Issues 2, 6) work correctly. Multi-select UI works (Issue 4). Create folder works (Issue 5). Panel folder operations mostly work (Issue 6). CRITICAL BUGS FOUND: (1) Alert.alert() is a NO-OP in React Native Web — all delete confirmation dialogs and success messages are non-functional in web preview (Issues 3,4,5,6 delete flows); (2) copy_items in server.py doesn't set image_type='panel' when copying to a folder — copied source images appear in Source Screenshots instead of target folder AND fail to serve (HTTP 404).

critical.issue: copy_items preserves original image_type when copying to a folder. A 'source' image copied to a folder gets image_type='source' instead of 'panel'. Causes: (1) file stored in PANELS_DIR but served from SOURCES_DIR → HTTP 404; (2) copied image not shown in folder detail view (which filters image_type='panel'); (3) copied image shows in Source Screenshots instead of target folder
critical.fix: In server.py copy_items(), set: copy_image_type = 'panel' if target_folder else doc['image_type']. File path logic also needs adjustment for 'source' type images in folders.

ui_bugs.issue: numColumns={2} renders panels with incorrect width in web preview (939px instead of ~167px). Each panel takes full screen width rather than 2-column layout. This is a React Native Web FlatList numColumns limitation.
ui_bugs.priority: LOW - visual only, functionality works; native mobile renders correctly

integration_issues.flow: Issue 7: Copy source screenshot to folder
integration_issues.issue: After copying a source image to a panel folder via the folder picker: (1) copy shows in Source Screenshots section instead of target folder in UI; (2) copy's file cannot be served (HTTP 404 on /api/images/{id}/file); (3) copy not visible when navigating into target folder
integration_issues.root_cause: copy_items() in server.py copies image_type from original. Source images in folders have mismatched image_type vs storage location.

react_native_issues.component: Alert.alert() - ALL screens using delete confirmation
react_native_issues.issue: Alert.alert() is a NO-OP in React Native Web (react-native-web implementation: class Alert { static alert() {} }). ALL delete confirmation dialogs (Issue 3: single delete, Issue 4: bulk delete, Issue 5: folder delete, Issue 6: panel delete + folder delete) are completely non-functional in the web preview. The delete API calls inside Alert's onPress callback are NEVER executed from web. On actual mobile (iOS/Android via Expo Go), this works correctly as Alert.alert() uses native dialogs.

action_items: CRITICAL FIX: server.py copy_items() - set copy image_type based on destination: `copy_image_type = 'panel' if target_folder else doc['image_type']` AND ensure file path logic is consistent

CRITICAL FIX: Alert.alert() is a no-op in React Native Web. For web compatibility, replace Alert.alert() delete confirmation flows with a custom Modal/Dialog component. Affects: index.tsx handleDelete(), folder/[id].tsx handleDeletePanels() and deleteThisFolder(). Alternative: use Platform.OS check to conditionally use native Alert on mobile vs custom dialog on web.
Consider: get_image_file in server.py - the file path lookup should handle the case where a source image has been moved/copied to a folder (file in PANELS_DIR but image_type='source')
critical_code_review_comments: server.py copy_items() line 336: `target_folder = req.target_folder_id if req.target_folder_id is not None else doc.get('folder_id')` - correctly handles target_folder_id being None vs not provided. But the copy's image_type should be 'panel' when target_folder is set.
Alert.alert() usage in index.tsx and folder/[id].tsx is correct for mobile but needs a web fallback (or custom modal) for web preview to work.
Issue 2 (Rename): FULLY FIXED - handleRenameAction correctly handles both 'folder' and 'image' types with correct modal titles ('Rename Folder' / 'Rename File')
Issue 6 (Panel folder view): FULLY IMPLEMENTED - selection mode, all 5 toolbar buttons, panel rename, folder rename via header, folder delete button, back navigation - all present and functional
Issue 4 (Multi-select): WORKS - long-press one item, tap others, selection count increments correctly to '3 selected', red border highlights on selected items
Panel grid layout: numColumns=2 in FlatList does not render as 2-column grid on React Native Web. Panel card width is 939px instead of ~167px. Not a blocking issue for mobile.

success_rate.frontend: 60% (Issues 2, 4 multi-select UI, 5 create, 6 navigation+rename fully working; Issues 3/4/5/6 delete flows blocked by Alert.alert no-op in RN Web; Issue 7 copy integration broken)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_iter4_new_folder during testing. Renamed 'hello' folder to 'hello_renamed_by_test'. Renamed source image to 'renamed_source_iter4.png'. Created Copy_renamed_source_iter4.png in TEST_iter4_new_folder (broken - shows in Source Screenshots instead of folder).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 4 found 2 critical bugs: (1) Alert.alert no-op in RN Web blocks all delete confirmation testing via web preview - need to either implement custom Alert or use Platform.OS check; (2) copy_items doesn't update image_type to 'panel' when copying to a folder - need backend fix. Issues 2 (rename images+folders), 4 (multi-select), 5 (create folder), 6 (navigation, rename panel, rename folder via header) are all working correctly. The folder/[id].tsx panel grid layout uses numColumns=2 but renders as single wide column in web preview (not a mobile bug).

issue_7_copy_bug.description: Copying a source image to a panel folder results in: copy appears in Source Screenshots, file serving fails (404), copy not visible in target folder
issue_7_copy_bug.root_cause: In server.py copy_items() line 356: `'image_type': doc['image_type']` copies the original's image_type. For source images, this stays 'source'. The file is stored at PANELS_DIR/target_folder/new_stored but get_image_file() checks `if doc['image_type'] == 'source'` and looks at SOURCES_DIR/new_stored (file not there → 404). Additionally, get_folder() queries `image_type: 'panel'` so source-type copies don't appear in folder view. And listImages('source') returns ALL source images including the copy (showing it in Source Screenshots).
issue_7_copy_bug.fix: In copy_items(), determine copy image_type based on destination: `copy_image_type = 'panel' if target_folder else doc['image_type']`. This ensures copies to folders are treated as panels and are findable/servable correctly.
issue_7_copy_bug.reproduction: 1. Long-press source screenshot → select → Copy → pick a folder → confirm. The copy appears in Source Screenshots instead of the target folder. Navigate to the target folder - copy is NOT visible. Try to load copy's image - HTTP 404.

alert_alert_no_op.description: All delete confirmation dialogs (handleDelete, handleDeletePanels, deleteThisFolder) are non-functional on React Native Web
alert_alert_no_op.root_cause: react-native-web implements Alert as a class with empty static alert() method. The web preview renders the app via react-native-web. The Alert.alert() onPress callbacks are never called, so the delete API calls never execute from web UI.
alert_alert_no_op.fix: Use a custom Modal-based confirmation dialog similar to the existing rename/create-folder modals, or use Platform.select() to conditionally use native Alert on mobile and a custom dialog on web.
alert_alert_no_op.reproduction: Open app in web preview. Long-press any item → enter selection mode → press Delete toolbar button. No confirmation dialog appears. Item is not deleted. The toolbar remains visible.
